political.s: REI as a company makes no direct contributions to candidates or super PACs, though employee-focused Outdoor Industry Association PAC operates separately. The verified FEC data showing lean-right donations (20,500 rep / 29,500 total) appears inconsistent with publicly stated company political neutrality policy and recent CEO reaffirmation of left-leaning values including DEI commitment despite national political pressure.

charity.s: REI launched the REI Cooperative Action Fund in 2021, a 501(c)(3) distributing approximately $31 million in grants since launch as of 2025. The Fund supports nonprofits promoting outdoor access, equity, and inclusion. REI ensures 100% of public donations reach grantees through corporate underwriting of operations.
charity.amt: $31 million cumulative since 2021; ~$7.2 million in spring 2025 cycle
charity.focus: Environmental access, outdoor equity, BIPOC community inclusion, conservation

environment.s: REI achieved Climate Label certification and committed to 55% GHG emissions reduction by 2030. Company exceeded 2025 recycled polyester goal (52% vs. 45% target), diverts 90% of waste from landfill, uses 70%+ Bluesign-approved fabrics, and pays Fair Trade Premiums. Leadership has embedded sustainability into supply chain standards and product design.

labor.s: REI faces significant labor challenges including OSHA fines for failing to provide respirator information and safety training in ski shops. NLRB complaint filed March 2025 alleging 175 labor law violations including withholding merit raises and bonuses from unionized workers. 11 stores have unionized since 2022 with no successful first contracts negotiated.

dei.s: REI has invested substantially in DEI initiatives since 2020, including establishing a Chief Diversity Officer, BIPOC Advisory Council, and mandatory employee education on racial equity. New CEO Mary Beth Laughton reaffirmed commitment to DEI in May 2025 while most retailers rolled back efforts. Glassdoor DEI rating 3.9/5 matches retail sector average.

animals.s: Not applicable to REI's business model as an outdoor retail company that does not manufacture cosmetics or pharmaceuticals requiring animal testing.

guns.s: REI does not sell guns or ammunition. In 2018, the company halted orders from Vista Outdoor and its subsidiaries until Vista addressed gun manufacturing connections, resuming in 2019 after Vista sold Savage Arms. REI supports hunters but believes gun manufacturers have responsibility to engage in gun safety discussions.

privacy.s: REI maintains formal privacy policies compliant with CCPA and state laws allowing user access, correction, and deletion of personal data. Company does not sell personal information to third parties. However, data collection practices for behavioral targeting and analytics are extensive, including location data and inference tracking.

execPay.s: REI targets executive base pay at median market rate for comparable roles; total target compensation typically positioned 25th-50th percentile. Compensation heavily weighted toward performance with annual and long-term incentive components. As a cooperative, no stock options available. Average executive compensation approximately $234K annually with documented pay ranging $50K-$700K.

fec.committeeName: REINSURANCE ASSOCIATION OF AMERICA POLITICAL ACTION COMMITTEE INC (REPAC)

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
